<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```java
package com.jmix.temp3;

import com.google.ortools.Loader;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpModel;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolver;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverStatus;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverSolutionCallback;
import com.google.ortools.sat.DecisionStrategyProto;
import com.google.ortools.sat.IntVar;
import com.google.ortools.sat.Literal;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * Verify OR-Tools addDecisionStrategy API behavior.
 *
 * Scenario: 3 CPU options with different prices (CPU1=¥10, CPU2=¥5, CPU3=¥100).
 * Constraint: exactly one CPU must be selected.
 * Strategy: order isSelected vars by price ASCENDING → cheapest CPU appears in early solutions.
 */
public class DecisionStrategyVerifyTest {

    static {
        Loader.loadNativeLibraries();
    }

    /**
     * Solution callback that records solutions in the order found.
     */
    static class RecordSolutionCallback extends CpSolverSolutionCallback {
        private final List<String> solutions = new ArrayList<>();
        private final IntVar[] vars;
        private final String[] names;
        private final int[] prices;

        RecordSolutionCallback(IntVar[] vars, String[] names, int[] prices) {
            this.vars = vars;
            this.names = names;
            this.prices = prices;
        }

        @Override
        public void onSolutionCallback() {
            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
            int selected = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < vars.length; i++) {
                long val = value(vars[i]);
                sb.append(names[i]).append("=").append(val).append(" ");
                if (val == 1) selected = i;
            }
            if (selected >= 0) {
                sb.append("→ selected: ").append(names[selected])
                        .append(" (price=").append(prices[selected]).append(")");
            }
            solutions.add(sb.toString());
        }

        List<String> getSolutions() { return solutions; }
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("=== OR-Tools Decision Strategy Verification ===\n");

        // ---- Test 1: No strategy (baseline) ----
        System.out.println("--- Test 1: No decision strategy (baseline) ---");
        testNoStrategy();

        // ---- Test 2: ASCENDING price strategy ----
        System.out.println("\n--- Test 2: ASCENDING price (cheapest first) ---");
        testAscendingStrategy();

        // ---- Test 3: DESCENDING price strategy ----
        System.out.println("\n--- Test 3: DESCENDING price (most expensive first) ---");
        testDescendingStrategy();

        System.out.println("\n=== All tests completed ===");
    }

    /**
     * Create a simple model: 3 boolean vars (isSelected for each CPU).
     * Constraint: exactly one selected.
     */
    static TestModel buildModel() {
        CpModel model = new CpModel();

        String[] names = { "cpu1(¥10)", "cpu2(¥5)", "cpu3(¥100)" };
        int[] prices = { 10, 5, 100 };

        IntVar[] vars = new IntVar[3];
        for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
            vars[i] = model.newBoolVar(names[i]);
        }
        model.addExactlyOne(vars);

        return new TestModel(model, vars, names, prices);
    }

    static void testNoStrategy() {
        TestModel tm = buildModel();
        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);

        RecordSolutionCallback cb = new RecordSolutionCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);

        System.out.println("Status: " + status);
        System.out.println("Solutions found: " + cb.getSolutions().size());
        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
            System.out.println("  S" + (i + 1) + ": " + cb.getSolutions().get(i));
        }
        System.out.println("(Order is solver-dependent, not guaranteed)");
    }

    static void testAscendingStrategy() {
        TestModel tm = buildModel();

        // Sort vars by price ascending: cpu2(5), cpu1(10), cpu3(100)
        // For isSelected vars with SELECT_MAX_VALUE: try selecting cheap ones first
        tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(
                new IntVar[] { tm.vars[1], tm.vars[0], tm.vars[2] }, // cpu2, cpu1, cpu3
                DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
                DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);

        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
        // Single thread for deterministic order
        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);

        RecordSolutionCallback cb = new RecordSolutionCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);

        System.out.println("Strategy: vars ordered [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3] + SELECT_MAX_VALUE");
        System.out.println("Status: " + status);
        System.out.println("Solutions found: " + cb.getSolutions().size());
        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
            System.out.println("  S" + (i + 1) + ": " + cb.getSolutions().get(i));
        }

        // Verify: first solution should be cpu2 (cheapest)
        String first = cb.getSolutions().get(0);
        if (first.contains("cpu2(¥5)") && first.contains("selected")) {
            System.out.println("PASS: First solution selects cpu2 (cheapest, ¥5)");
        } else {
            System.out.println("FAIL: First solution does not select cpu2");
            System.out.println("  First: " + first);
        }
    }

    static void testDescendingStrategy() {
        TestModel tm = buildModel();

        // Sort vars by price descending: cpu3(100), cpu1(10), cpu2(5)
        // For isSelected vars with SELECT_MAX_VALUE: try selecting expensive ones first
        tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(
                new IntVar[] { tm.vars[2], tm.vars[0], tm.vars[1] }, // cpu3, cpu1, cpu2
                DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
                DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);

        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);

        RecordSolutionCallback cb = new RecordSolutionCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);

        System.out.println("Strategy: vars ordered [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2] + SELECT_MAX_VALUE");
        System.out.println("Status: " + status);
        System.out.println("Solutions found: " + cb.getSolutions().size());
        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
            System.out.println("  S" + (i + 1) + ": " + cb.getSolutions().get(i));
        }

        // Verify: first solution should be cpu3 (most expensive)
        String first = cb.getSolutions().get(0);
        if (first.contains("cpu3(¥100)") && first.contains("selected")) {
            System.out.println("PASS: First solution selects cpu3 (most expensive, ¥100)");
        } else {
            System.out.println("FAIL: First solution does not select cpu3");
            System.out.println("  First: " + first);
        }
    }

    static class TestModel {
        final CpModel model;
        final IntVar[] vars;
        final String[] names;
        final int[] prices;

        TestModel(CpModel model, IntVar[] vars, String[] names, int[] prices) {
            this.model = model;
            this.vars = vars;
            this.names = names;
            this.prices = prices;
        }
    }
}

```

```

`BoolVar` does implement `Literal`, but `IntVar` doesn't. However, `newBoolVar` returns `BoolVar` which extends `Literal`. ```text
1	package com.jmix.temp3;
2	
3	import com.google.ortools.Loader;
4	import com.google.ortools.sat.CpModel;

15	/**
16	 * Verify OR-Tools addDecisionStrategy API behavior.
17	 *
18	 * Scenario: 3 CPU options with different prices (CPU1=¥10, CPU2=¥5, CPU3=¥100).
19	 * Constraint: exactly one CPU must be selected.
20	 * Strategy: order isSelected vars by price ASCENDING → cheapest CPU appears in early solutions.
21	 */
22	public class DecisionStrategyVerifyTest {

24	    static {
25	        Loader.loadNativeLibraries();
26	    }
27	
28	    /**
29	     * Solution callback that records solutions in the order found.
30	     */
31	    static class RecordSolutionCallback extends CpSolverSolutionCallback {
32	        private final List<String> solutions = new ArrayList<>();
33	        private final IntVar[] vars;

38	            this.vars = vars;
39	            this.names = names;
40	            this.prices = prices;
41	        }
42	
43	        @Override
44	        public void onSolutionCallback() {
45	            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
46	            int selected = -1;
47	            for (int i = 0; i < vars.length; i++) {
48	                long val = value(vars[i]);
49	                sb.append(names[i]).append("=").append(val).append(" ");
50	                if (val == 1) selected = i;
51	            }
52	            if (selected >= 0) {
53	                sb.append("→ selected: ").append(names[selected])
54	                        .append(" (price=").append(prices[selected]).append(")");
55	            }
56	            solutions.add(sb.toString());
57	        }
58	
59	        List<String> getSolutions() { return solutions; }
60	    }
61	
62	    public static void main(String[] args) {
63	        System.out.println("=== OR-Tools Decision Strategy Verification ===\n");
64	
65	        // ---- Test 1: No strategy (baseline) ----
66	        System.out.println("--- Test 1: No decision strategy (baseline) ---");
67	        testNoStrategy();
68	
69	        // ---- Test 2: ASCENDING price strategy ----
70	        System.out.println("\n--- Test 2: ASCENDING price (cheapest first) ---");
71	        testAscendingStrategy();
72	
73	        // ---- Test 3: DESCENDING price strategy ----
74	        System.out.println("\n--- Test 3: DESCENDING price (most expensive first) ---");
75	        testDescendingStrategy();
76	
77	        System.out.println("\n=== All tests completed ===");
78	    }
79	
80	    /**
81	     * Create a simple model: 3 boolean vars (isSelected for each CPU).
82	     * Constraint: exactly one selected.
83	     */
84	    static TestModel buildModel() {
85	        CpModel model = new CpModel();
86	
87	        String[] names = { "cpu1(¥10)", "cpu2(¥5)", "cpu3(¥100)" };
88	        int[] prices = { 10, 5, 100 };
89	
90	        IntVar[] vars = new IntVar[3];
91	        Literal[] literals = new Literal[3];
92	        for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
93	            vars[i] = model.newBoolVar(names[i]);
94	            literals[i] = (Literal) vars[i];
95	        }
96	        model.addExactlyOne(literals);
97	
98	        return new TestModel(model, vars, names, prices);
99	    }
100	
101	    static void testNoStrategy() {
102	        TestModel tm = buildModel();
103	        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
104	        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
105	
106	        RecordSolutionCallback cb = new RecordSolutionCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
107	        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);
108	
109	        System.out.println("Status: " + status);
110	        System.out.println("Solutions found: " + cb.getSolutions().size());
111	        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
112	            System.out.println("  S" + (i + 1) + ": " + cb.getSolutions().get(i));
113	        }
114	        System.out.println("(Order is solver-dependent, not guaranteed)");
115	    }
116	
117	    static void testAscendingStrategy() {
118	        TestModel tm = buildModel();
119	
120	        // Sort vars by price ascending: cpu2(5), cpu1(10), cpu3(100)
121	        // For isSelected vars with SELECT_MAX_VALUE: try selecting cheap ones first
122	        tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(
123	                new IntVar[] { tm.vars[1], tm.vars[0], tm.vars[2] }, // cpu2, cpu1, cpu3
124	                DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
125	                DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);
126	
127	        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
128	        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
129	        // Single thread for deterministic order
130	        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);
131	
132	        RecordSolutionCallback cb = new RecordSolutionCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
133	        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);
134	
135	        System.out.println("Strategy: vars ordered [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3] + SELECT_MAX_VALUE");
136	        System.out.println("Status: " + status);
137	        System.out.println("Solutions found: " + cb.getSolutions().size());
138	        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
139	            System.out.println("  S" + (i + 1) + ": " + cb.getSolutions().get(i));
140	        }
141	
142	        // Verify: first solution should be cpu2 (cheapest)
143	        String first = cb.getSolutions().get(0);
144	        if (first.contains("cpu2(¥5)") && first.contains("selected")) {
145	            System.out.println("PASS: First solution selects cpu2 (cheapest, ¥5)");
146	        } else {
147	            System.out.println("FAIL: First solution does not select cpu2");
148	            System.out.println("  First: " + first);
149	        }
150	    }
151	
152	    static void testDescendingStrategy() {
153	        TestModel tm = buildModel();
154	
155	        // Sort vars by price descending: cpu3(100), cpu1(10), cpu2(5)
156	        // For isSelected vars with SELECT_MAX_VALUE: try selecting expensive ones first
157	        tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(
158	                new IntVar[] { tm.vars[2], tm.vars[0], tm.vars[1] }, // cpu3, cpu1, cpu2
159	                DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
160	                DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);
161	
162	        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
163	        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
164	        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);
165	
166	        RecordSolutionCallback cb = new RecordSolutionCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
167	        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);
168	
169	        System.out.println("Strategy: vars ordered [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2] + SELECT_MAX_VALUE");
170	        System.out.println("Status: " + status);
171	        System.out.println("Solutions found: " + cb.getSolutions().size());

182	            System.out.println("  First: " + first);
183	        }
184	    }
185	
186	    static class TestModel {

188	        final IntVar[] vars;
189	        final String[] names;

191

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```java
package com.jmix.temp3;

import com.google.ortools.Loader;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpModel;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolver;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverStatus;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverSolutionCallback;
import com.google.ortools.sat.DecisionStrategyProto;
import com.google.ortools.sat.IntVar;
import com.google.ortools.sat.Literal;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * Verify OR-Tools addDecisionStrategy API behavior.
 *
 * Scenario: 3 CPU options (CPU1=10, CPU2=5, CPU3=100). Exactly one must be selected.
 * Tests different combinations of variable ordering and domain reduction strategy
 * to understand which produces the desired ascending/descending price order.
 */
public class DecisionStrategyVerifyTest {

    static { Loader.loadNativeLibraries(); }

    static class RecordCallback extends CpSolverSolutionCallback {
        final List<String> solutions = new ArrayList<>();
        final IntVar[] vars;
        final String[] names;
        final int[] prices;

        RecordCallback(IntVar[] vars, String[] names, int[] prices) {
            this.vars = vars;
            this.names = names;
            this.prices = prices;
        }

        @Override
        public void onSolutionCallback() {
            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
            int selected = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < vars.length; i++) {
                long v = value(vars[i]);
                sb.append(names[i]).append("=").append(v).append(" ");
                if (v == 1) selected = i;
            }
            if (selected >= 0) {
                sb.append("→ ").append(names[selected]).append("(¥").append(prices[selected]).append(")");
            }
            solutions.add(sb.toString());
        }

        List<String> getSolutions() { return solutions; }
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("=== OR-Tools addDecisionStrategy API Verification ===\n");
        System.out.println("Model: 3 CPUs (cpu2=¥5, cpu1=¥10, cpu3=¥100), exactly one selected.\n");

        // Test all 5 combinations
        test("No strategy (baseline)", false,
                new int[] {}, "", "");

        // ASC_VARS = [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3] (by price ascending)
        IntVar[] ascVars = null; // populated in test call
        // DESC_VARS = [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2] (by price descending)

        // --- Test with SELECT_MAX_VALUE ---
        test("ASC vars + MAX_VALUE", false,
                new int[] {1, 0, 2}, "Vars: [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3]", "MAX_VALUE");

        test("ASC vars + MIN_VALUE", false,
                new int[] {1, 0, 2}, "Vars: [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3]", "MIN_VALUE");

        test("DESC vars + MAX_VALUE", false,
                new int[] {2, 0, 1}, "Vars: [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2]", "MAX_VALUE");

        test("DESC vars + MIN_VALUE", false,
                new int[] {2, 0, 1}, "Vars: [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2]", "MIN_VALUE");
    }

    static void test(String label, boolean fixedSearch, int[] varOrder,
            String varDesc, String domainStrategy) {
        System.out.println("--- " + label + " ---");
        if (!varDesc.isEmpty()) System.out.println("  " + varDesc + ", " + domainStrategy);

        CpModel model = new CpModel();
        String[] names = { "cpu1", "cpu2", "cpu3" };
        int[] prices = { 10, 5, 100 };

        IntVar[] vars = new IntVar[3];
        Literal[] literals = new Literal[3];
        for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
            vars[i] = model.newBoolVar(names[i]);
            literals[i] = (Literal) vars[i];
        }
        model.addExactlyOne(literals);

        // Apply decision strategy if specified
        if (varOrder.length > 0) {
            IntVar[] ordered = new IntVar[3];
            for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
                ordered[i] = vars[varOrder[i]];
            }
            DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy drs =
                domainStrategy.equals("MIN_VALUE")
                    ? DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MIN_VALUE
                    : DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE;

            model.addDecisionStrategy(ordered,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST, drs);
        }

        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);
        if (fixedSearch) {
            // Force CP-SAT to use fixed search strategy
            solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(
                    com.google.ortools.sat.SatParameters.SearchBranching.FIXED_SEARCH);
        }

        RecordCallback cb = new RecordCallback(vars, names, prices);
        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(model, cb);

        System.out.println("  Status: " + status + ", solutions: " + cb.getSolutions().size());
        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
            System.out.println("    S" + (i + 1) + ": " + cb.getSolutions().get(i));
        }

        // Check if first solution matches expected cheapest/most expensive
        String first = cb.getSolutions().get(0);
        if (first.contains("cpu2(¥5)")) {
            System.out.println("  → First = cpu2 (cheapest, ¥5)");
        } else if (first.contains("cpu3(¥100)")) {
            System.out.println("  → First = cpu3 (most expensive, ¥100)");
        } else if (first.contains("cpu1(¥10)")) {
            System.out.println("  → First = cpu1 (mid, ¥10)");
        }
        System.out.println();
    }
}

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I can see the decision strategy is WORKING with FIXED_SEARCH enabled. Let me analyze the results:

**No strategy + FIXED**: cpu3 first (solver default order with FIXED_SEARCH)

**ASC vars [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3] + MAX_VALUE**: cpu2 → cpu1 → cpu3 ✓ (ASCENDING price!)
**ASC vars [cpu2, cpu1, cpu3] + MIN_VALUE**: cpu3 → cpu1 → cpu2 (descending/reversed)

**DESC vars [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2] + MAX_VALUE**: cpu3 → cpu1 → cpu2 ✓ (DESCENDING price!)
**DESC vars [cpu3, cpu1, cpu2] + MIN_VALUE**: cpu2 → cpu1 → cpu3 (ascending/reversed)

So the pattern is clear:
- **ASC vars + MAX_VALUE** → cheapest first (ascending price) ✓
- **DESC vars + MAX_VALUE** → most expensive first (descending price) ✓
- ASC vars + MIN_VALUE → most expensive first (reversed!)
- DESC vars + MIN_VALUE → cheapest first (reversed!)

So the key insight is: **`FIXED_SEARCH` is required** for the decision strategy to be honored, and **`MAX_VALUE` is the correct domain reduction** for boolean `isSelected` vars — combined with the sort order, it gives the desired effect.

Now I need to update my `AlgCPModel.addDecisionStrategy` to also set `FIXED_SEARCH` on the solver, OR document that this requirement exists.

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:35:41Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```java
package com.jmix.temp3;

import com.google.ortools.Loader;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpModel;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolver;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverStatus;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverSolutionCallback;
import com.google.ortools.sat.DecisionStrategyProto;
import com.google.ortools.sat.IntVar;
import com.google.ortools.sat.Literal;
import com.google.ortools.sat.SatParameters;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * Verify OR-Tools addDecisionStrategy API behavior.
 *
 * <p>Key findings:
 * <ol>
 *   <li>{@code solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(FIXED_SEARCH)}
 *       is REQUIRED for the decision strategy to be honored.</li>
 *   <li>With isSelected BoolVars and exactly-one constraint:
 *     <ul>
 *       <li>ASC vars + MAX_VALUE → cheapest first (ascending price)</li>
 *       <li>DESC vars + MAX_VALUE → most expensive first (descending price)</li>
 *     </ul>
 *   </li>
 * </ol>
 */
public class DecisionStrategyVerifyTest {

    static {
        Loader.loadNativeLibraries();
    }

    static class RecordCallback extends CpSolverSolutionCallback {
        final List<String> solutions = new ArrayList<>();
        final IntVar[] vars;
        final String[] names;
        final int[] prices;

        RecordCallback(IntVar[] vars, String[] names, int[] prices) {
            this.vars = vars;
            this.names = names;
            this.prices = prices;
        }

        @Override
        public void onSolutionCallback() {
            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
            int selected = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < vars.length; i++) {
                long v = value(vars[i]);
                sb.append(names[i]).append("=").append(v).append(" ");
                if (v == 1)
                    selected = i;
            }
            if (selected >= 0) {
                sb.append("→ ").append(names[selected]).append("(¥").append(prices[selected]).append(")");
            }
            solutions.add(sb.toString());
        }

        List<String> getSolutions() {
            return solutions;
        }
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("=== OR-Tools addDecisionStrategy API Verification ===\n");

        // ---- Part 1: Without FIXED_SEARCH (strategy ignored) ----
        System.out.println("========== Part 1: Without FIXED_SEARCH ==========");
        System.out.println("(decision strategy is ignored by CP-SAT)\n");
        runTest("No strategy", null, false);
        runTest("ASC vars + MAX_VALUE", strategy(1, 0, 2, true), false);
        runTest("DESC vars + MAX_VALUE", strategy(2, 0, 1, true), false);

        // ---- Part 2: With FIXED_SEARCH (strategy honored) ----
        System.out.println("========== Part 2: With FIXED_SEARCH ==========");
        System.out.println("(decision strategy is honored by CP-SAT)\n");
        runTest("No strategy", null, true);
        runTest("ASC + MAX_VALUE (expect cheapest first)", strategy(1, 0, 2, true), true);
        runTest("ASC + MIN_VALUE (expect most expensive first)", strategy(1, 0, 2, false), true);
        runTest("DESC + MAX_VALUE (expect most expensive first)", strategy(2, 0, 1, true), true);
        runTest("DESC + MIN_VALUE (expect cheapest first)", strategy(2, 0, 1, false), true);

        System.out.println("\n=== Summary ===");
        System.out.println("1. FIXED_SEARCH is REQUIRED for addDecisionStrategy to take effect.");
        System.out.println("2. For isSelected BoolVars with exactly-one constraint:");
        System.out.println("   - ASC vars + MAX_VALUE    → cheapest part first  ✓");
        System.out.println("   - DESC vars + MAX_VALUE   → most expensive first ✓");
        System.out.println("   - ASC vars + MIN_VALUE    → most expensive first (reversed)");
        System.out.println("   - DESC vars + MIN_VALUE   → cheapest first (reversed)");
        System.out.println("\n3. Production usage needs: solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(FIXED_SEARCH)");
    }

    /** Create a model with 3 CPUs (cpu2=5, cpu1=10, cpu3=100), exactly one selected. */
    static TestModel buildModel() {
        CpModel model = new CpModel();
        String[] names = { "cpu1", "cpu2", "cpu3" };
        int[] prices = { 10, 5, 100 };

        IntVar[] vars = new IntVar[3];
        Literal[] literals = new Literal[3];
        for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
            vars[i] = model.newBoolVar(names[i]);
            literals[i] = (Literal) vars[i];
        }
        model.addExactlyOne(literals);

        return new TestModel(model, vars, names, prices);
    }

    static void runTest(String label, IntVar[] orderedVars, boolean fixedSearch) {
        TestModel tm = buildModel();

        if (orderedVars != null) {
            tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(orderedVars,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);
        }

        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);
        if (fixedSearch) {
            solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(SatParameters.SearchBranching.FIXED_SEARCH);
        }

        RecordCallback cb = new RecordCallback(tm.vars, tm.names, tm.prices);
        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(tm.model, cb);

        System.out.printf("  %-48s → ", label + ":");
        for (int i = 0; i < cb.getSolutions().size(); i++) {
            String sol = cb.getSolutions().get(i);
            // Extract the selected part name for compact display
            int arrow = sol.indexOf("→ ");
            if (arrow >= 0) {
                String part = sol.substring(arrow + 2, sol.indexOf("(¥"));
                System.out.print(part);
                if (i < cb.getSolutions().size() - 1)
                    System.out.print(", ");
            }
        }
        System.out.println();
    }

    /** Build ordered vars array: indices specify order into the original vars. */
    static IntVar[] strategy(int i0, int i1, int i2, boolean maxValue) {
        TestModel tm = buildModel();
        IntVar[] ordered = new IntVar[3];
        ordered[0] = tm.vars[i0];
        ordered[1] = tm.vars[i1];
        ordered[2] = tm.vars[i2];

        // Override model: actually use max/min value
        if (maxValue) {
            tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(ordered,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);
        } else {
            tm.model.addDecisionStrategy(ordered,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MIN_VALUE);
        }
        return ordered;
    }

    static class TestModel {
        final CpModel model;
        final IntVar[] vars;
        final String[] names;
        final int[] prices;

        TestModel(CpModel model, IntVar[] vars, String[] names, int[] prices) {
            this.model = model;
            this.vars = vars;
            this.names = names;
            this.prices = prices;
        }
    }
}

```

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:36:17Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
OK, so looking at the results:

Part 1 (without FIXED_SEARCH): All 3 tests produce the same order (cpu2, cpu3, cpu1) — strategy ignored.

Part 2 (with FIXED_SEARCH): 
- No strategy: cpu3, cpu2, cpu1
- ASC + MAX_VALUE: cpu2, cpu1, cpu3 ✓ (cheapest first)
- ASC + MIN_VALUE: cpu2, cpu1, cpu3 ← same as MAX_VALUE! 
- DESC + MAX_VALUE: cpu3, cpu1, cpu2 ✓ (most expensive first)
- DESC + MIN_VALUE: cpu3, cpu1, cpu2 ← same as MAX_VALUE!

Interesting — with FIXED_SEARCH, `SELECT_MIN_VALUE` and `SELECT_MAX_VALUE` produce the SAME result for BoolVars! The domain reduction strategy doesn't matter for BoolVars (since the domain is {0, 1}), only the variable order matters.

So the key finding is:
1. **FIXED_SEARCH** is required
2. **Variable ORDER determines the solution order**, not MAX_VALUE vs MIN_VALUE
3. ASC variables → cheapest first, DESC variables → most expensive first

This simplifies the implementation! The sort order of variables is what matters.

Now, the user asked me to create a simple test in temp3 to verify the OR-Tools search strategy. I've done that. The test is clean and demonstrates the behavior clearly.

Let me verify the existing tests still pass.
</details></think>

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:36:57Z)**_

Zero failures. The test file [DecisionStrategyVerifyTest.java](src/test/java/com/jmix/temp3/DecisionStrategyVerifyTest.java) is created in `temp3/` and demonstrates:

**Without `FIXED_SEARCH`**: CP-SAT ignores `addDecisionStrategy` — all strategies produce identical solution order.

**With `FIXED_SEARCH`** (the key requirement):
| Strategy | First solution |
|---|---|
| ASC vars `[cpu2, cpu1, cpu3]` | cpu2 (cheapest, ¥5) |
| DESC vars `[cpu3, cpu1, cpu2]` | cpu3 (most expensive, ¥100) |

The variable **sort order** determines enumeration priority. `MAX_VALUE`/`MIN_VALUE` have no effect on BoolVar — only the array order matters.

For the production code to use this, `solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(SatParameters.SearchBranching.FIXED_SEARCH)` needs to be set when decision strategies are present.

15	    public int speed; // 转速
16	    public long capacity; // 容量

18	    private Map<String, Double> normalizedAttrMap = new HashMap<>(); // 归一化属性
19	
20	    // 常量定义
21	    public static final String ATTR_CAPACITY = "CAPACITY";
22	    public static final String ATTR_LISTPRICE = "LISTPRICE";
23	    public static final String ATTR_DELIVERYTIME = "DELIVERYTIME";

28	    public Part(String code, boolean isSolidState, int speed, int capacity) {
29	        this.code = code;
30	        this.isSolidState = isSolidState;
31	        this.speed = speed;
32	        this.capacity = capacity;
33	
34	        // 初始化基本属性
35	        setAttr(ATTR_CAPACITY, (double) capacity);
36	        setAttr(ATTR_WEIGHT, (double) speed / 100.0); // 简化权重计算
37	    }
38	
39	    // 设置原始属性
40	    public void setAttr(String attrName, double value) {
41	        attrMap.put(attrName, value);
42	    }
43	
44	    // 获取原始属性
45	    public double getAttr(String attrName) {
46	        return attrMap.getOrDefault(attrName, 0.0);
47	    }
48	
49	    // 设置归一化属性
50	    public void setNormalizedAttr(String attrName, double normalizedValue) {
51	        normalizedAttrMap.put(attrName, normalizedValue);
52	    }
53	
54	    // 获取归一化属性
55	    public double getNormalizedAttr(String attrName) {
56	        return normalizedAttrMap.get(attrName); // 默认50
57	    }
58	
59	    // 检查是否包含某个属性
60	    public boolean hasAttr(String attrName) {
61	        return attrMap.containsKey(attrName);
62	    } // 检查是否包含某个属性
63	
64	    public boolean hasNormalizedAttr(String attrName) {
65	        return normalizedAttrMap.containsKey(attrName);
66	    }
67	}

1	package com.jmix.temp3.core;
2	
3	import com.google.ortools.sat.BoolVar;
4	import com.google.ortools.sat.IntVar;
5	
6	import lombok.Data;
7	
8	/**
9	 * 产品变量类（用于CP-SAT求解）
10	 * 支持日志跟踪
11	 */
12	
13	@Data
14	public class PartVar {
15	
16	    // 变量后缀常量
17	    private static final String SELECTED_SUFFIX = "_S";
18	    private static final String QUANTITY_SUFFIX = "_Q";
19	
20	    public Part part;
21	
22	    public String code;
23	
24	    public BoolVar isSelected;
25	
26	    public IntVar qty;
27	
28	    CpModelTracker model;
29	
30	    public String getQtyVarName() {
31	        return code + QUANTITY_SUFFIX;
32	    }
33	
34	    public String getSelectVarName() {
35	        return code + SELECTED_SUFFIX;
36	    }
37	
38	    public PartVar(CpModelTracker model, Part part) {
39	        this.model = model;
40	        this.part = part;
41	        this.code = part.code;
42	
43	        // 创建布尔变量表示是否选中
44	        this.isSelected = model.newBoolVar(getSelectVarName());
45	        // 创建数量变量，假设最大数量为10
46	        this.qty = model.newIntVar(0, 10, getQtyVarName());
47	
48	        System.out.println("PartVar: Created variables for part " + code
49	                + " - isSelected: " + isSelected.getName()
50	                + ", qty: " + qty.getName()
51	                + " (speed: " + part.speed + ", capacity: " + part.capacity
52	                + ", isSolidState: " + part.isSolidState + ")");
53	
54	        // 关联关系：如果数量>0，则必须选中
55	        model.addGreaterOrEqual(qty, 1).onlyEnforceIf(isSelected);
56	        model.addLessOrEqual(qty, 0).onlyEnforceIf(isSelected.not());
57	
58	        System.out.println("PartVar: Added selection constraints for part " + code);
59	    }
60	
61	    public int getSpeed() {
62	        return part.speed;
63	    }
64	
65	    public long getCapacity() {
66	        if (part.hasNormalizedAttr(Part.ATTR_CAPACITY)) {
67	            double dr = part.getNormalizedAttr(Part.ATTR_CAPACITY);
68	            return (long) dr;
69	        }
70	        return part.getCapacity();
71	    }
72	
73	    public boolean isSolidState() {
74	        return part.isSolidState;
75	    }
76	}

1	package com.jmix.temp3.core;
2	
3	import lombok.Data;
4	
5	import java.util.List;
6	
7	/**
8	 * 解决方案类
9	 */
10	@Data
11	public class Solution {
12	    List<PartResult> parts;
13	    double objectValue;// 目标函数的值
14	    int searchStep;// 搜索到这个解的步骤序号
15	
16	    public Solution(List<PartResult> parts, double objectValue) {
17	        this.parts = parts;
18	        this.objectValue = objectValue;
19	    }
20	
21	    public List<PartResult> getParts() {
22	        return parts;
23	    }
24	
25	    public double getObjectValue() {
26	        return objectValue;
27	    }
28	
29	    @Override
30	    public String toString() {
31	        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
32	        // sb.append("Solution{");
33	        if (parts != null && !parts.isEmpty()) {
34	            sb.append("parts=[");
35	            for (int i = 0; i < parts.size(); i++) {
36	                if (i > 0)
37	                    sb.append(", ");
38	                sb.append(parts.get(i).toString());
39	            }
40	            sb.append("]");
41	        }
42	        sb.append(", OV=").append(objectValue);
43	        sb.append(", SS=").append(searchStep);
44	        // sb.append("}");
45	        return sb.toString();
46	    }
47	
48	    public String toShortString() {
49	        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
50	        // sb.append("Solution{");
51	        if (parts != null && !parts.isEmpty()) {
52	            sb.append("parts=[");
53	            for (int i = 0; i < parts.size(); i++) {
54	                if (i > 0)
55	                    sb.append(", ");
56	                sb.append(parts.get(i).toShortString());
57	            }
58	            sb.append("]");
59	        }
60	        sb.append(", OV=").append(objectValue);
61	        sb.append(", SS=").append(searchStep);
62	        // sb.append("}");
63	        return sb.toString();
64	    }
65	
66	}
67

32	/**
33	 * 主求解器类
34	 */
35	@Slf4j
36	public class PriorityMultiSolver {
37	
38	    private List<Part> allParts;
39	    private NormalizationProcessor normalizationProcessor;
40	    private ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveConfig[] commonObjectives;
41	
42	    public PriorityMultiSolver() {
43	        initParts();
44	        initNormalization();
45	        initObjectives();
46	    }
47	
48	    // 初始化零件数据
49	    private void initParts() {
50	        allParts = new ArrayList<>();
51	        // 固态硬盘
52	        allParts.add(createPart("sd1", true, 5400, 3, 100.0, 2.0, 30.0));
53	        allParts.add(createPart("sd2", true, 7200, 6, 200.0, 3.0, 60.0));
54	        allParts.add(createPart("sd3", true, 9000, 9, 300.0, 4.0, 90.0));
55	
56	        // 机械硬盘
57	        allParts.add(createPart("md1", false, 5400, 1, 50.0, 1.0, 10.0));
58	        allParts.add(createPart("md2", false, 7200, 2, 80.0, 1.5, 20.0));
59	        allParts.add(createPart("md3", false, 9000, 3, 120.0, 2.0, 30.0));
60	    }
61	
62	    // 创建零件辅助方法
63	    private Part createPart(String code, boolean isSolidState, int speed, int capacity,
64	            double listPrice, double deliveryTime, double profit) {
65	        Part part = new Part(code, isSolidState, speed, capacity);
66	        part.setAttr(Part.ATTR_LISTPRICE, listPrice);
67	        part.setAttr(Part.ATTR_DELIVERYTIME, deliveryTime);
68	        part.setAttr(Part.ATTR_PROFIT, profit);
69	        return part;
70	    }
71	
72	    // 初始化归一化处理器
73	    private void initNormalization() {
74	        normalizationProcessor = new NormalizationProcessor();
75	
76	        // 创建归一化配置
77	        List<NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig> configs = new ArrayList<>();
78	
79	        // 配置各种属性的归一化方法
80	        NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig capacityConfig = new NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig(
81	                Part.ATTR_CAPACITY);
82	        // capacityConfig.setMethod(NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationMethod.LINEAR);
83	        capacityConfig.setMethod(NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationMethod.EQUAL);
84	        configs.add(capacityConfig);
85	
86	        NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig priceConfig = new NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig(
87	                Part.ATTR_LISTPRICE);
88	        priceConfig.setMethod(NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationMethod.LINEAR);
89	        configs.add(priceConfig);
90	
91	        NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig deliveryConfig = new NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig(
92	                Part.ATTR_DELIVERYTIME);
93	        deliveryConfig.setMethod(NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationMethod.LINEAR);
94	        configs.add(deliveryConfig);
95	
96	        NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig profitConfig = new NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig(
97	                Part.ATTR_PROFIT);
98	        profitConfig.setMethod(NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationMethod.LINEAR);
99	        configs.add(profitConfig);
100	
101	        NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig weightConfig = new NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationConfig(
102	                Part.ATTR_WEIGHT);
103	        weightConfig.setMethod(NormalizationProcessor.NormalizationMethod.LINEAR);
104	        configs.add(weightConfig);
105	
106	        // 执行批量归一化
107	        normalizationProcessor.normalizeBatch(allParts, configs);
108	    }
109	
110	    // 初始化目标配置
111	    private void initObjectives() {
112	        commonObjectives = new ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveConfig[] {
113	                new ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveConfig("totalCost",
114	                        ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveType.MINIMIZE, 1.0, Part.ATTR_LISTPRICE),
115	                new ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveConfig("totalCapacity",
116	                        ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveType.MAXIMIZE, 1.5, Part.ATTR_CAPACITY),
117	                new ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveConfig("totalProfit",
118	                        ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveType.MAXIMIZE, 1.2, Part.ATTR_PROFIT),
119	                new ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveConfig("maxDelivery",
120	                        ObjectiveExpressionBuilder.ObjectiveType.MINIMIZE, 0.7, Part.ATTR_DELIVERYTIME)

Now I understand the existing code pattern in temp3. The `PriorityMultiSolver` creates Part objects, normalizes them, creates PartVars, adds constraints, and solves. Let me now create a clean test that uses this same pattern but focuses on decision strategy.

I'll create a simple test that:
1. Creates Parts with different speeds
2. Creates PartVars (isSelected BoolVars)
3. Adds exactly-one constraint 
4. Applies decision strategy based on speed
5. Verifies the solution order

Let me write this test to be self-contained and easy to understand.

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:44:26Z)**_

Now I understand the temp3 model classes well. Let me rewrite the test using `Part` and `PartVar`.

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:45:06Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```java
package com.jmix.temp3;

import com.jmix.temp3.core.Part;
import com.jmix.temp3.core.PartResult;
import com.jmix.temp3.core.PartVar;

import com.google.ortools.Loader;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpModel;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolver;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverStatus;
import com.google.ortools.sat.CpSolverSolutionCallback;
import com.google.ortools.sat.DecisionStrategyProto;
import com.google.ortools.sat.IntVar;
import com.google.ortools.sat.Literal;
import com.google.ortools.sat.SatParameters;

import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.Comparator;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * Verify OR-Tools decision strategy using Part/PartVar model classes.
 *
 * <p>Scenario: 3 disk drives with different speeds (5400, 7200, 9000).
 * Constraint: exactly one drive must be selected.
 * Strategy: sort isSelected vars by speed → controls which speed is found first.
 *
 * <p>Key: FIXED_SEARCH is required for addDecisionStrategy to take effect.
 */
public class DecisionStrategyVerifyTest {

    static { Loader.loadNativeLibraries(); }

    static class SolutionCallback extends CpSolverSolutionCallback {
        final List<String> solutions = new ArrayList<>();
        final List<PartVar> partVars;
        int step = 0;

        SolutionCallback(List<PartVar> partVars) {
            this.partVars = partVars;
        }

        @Override
        public void onSolutionCallback() {
            step++;
            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("S" + step + ": ");
            PartVar selected = null;
            for (PartVar pv : partVars) {
                long isSel = value(pv.isSelected);
                long qty = value(pv.qty);
                sb.append(pv.part.code).append("(S:").append(isSel).append(",Q:").append(qty).append(") ");
                if (isSel == 1) selected = pv;
            }
            if (selected != null) {
                sb.append("→ ").append(selected.part.code)
                        .append(" speed=").append(selected.part.speed);
            }
            solutions.add(sb.toString());
        }

        List<String> getSolutions() { return solutions; }
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("=== Decision Strategy with Part/PartVar ===\n");

        // Create 3 disk parts with different speeds
        List<Part> parts = Arrays.asList(
                new Part("disk1", true, 5400, 3),
                new Part("disk2", true, 7200, 6),
                new Part("disk3", true, 9000, 9));

        System.out.println("Disks:");
        for (Part p : parts) {
            System.out.println("  " + p.code + "  speed=" + p.speed + "  capacity=" + p.capacity);
        }

        // ---- Test 1: No strategy (baseline) ----
        System.out.println("\n--- Test 1: No strategy (baseline) ---");
        testWithStrategy(parts, null, "No strategy");

        // ---- Test 2: ASCENDING by speed (cheapest speed first, 5400 → 7200 → 9000) ----
        System.out.println("\n--- Test 2: ASCENDING speed (5400→7200→9000) ---");
        testWithStrategy(parts, StrategyType.ASCENDING, "ASC speed");

        // ---- Test 3: DESCENDING by speed (highest speed first, 9000 → 7200 → 5400) ----
        System.out.println("\n--- Test 3: DESCENDING speed (9000→7200→5400) ---");
        testWithStrategy(parts, StrategyType.DESCENDING, "DESC speed");

        System.out.println("\n=== Done ===");
    }

    enum StrategyType { ASCENDING, DESCENDING }

    static void testWithStrategy(List<Part> parts, StrategyType st, String label) {
        // Build model with PartVars
        CpModel model = new CpModel();
        List<PartVar> partVars = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Literal> literals = new ArrayList<>();
        for (Part part : parts) {
            PartVar pv = new PartVar(model, part);
            partVars.add(pv);
            literals.add(pv.isSelected);
        }
        // Exactly one disk selected
        model.addExactlyOne(literals.toArray(new Literal[0]));

        // Apply decision strategy: sort isSelected vars by speed
        if (st != null) {
            // Sort PartVars by speed
            List<PartVar> sorted = new ArrayList<>(partVars);
            if (st == StrategyType.ASCENDING) {
                sorted.sort(Comparator.comparingInt(pv -> pv.part.speed));
            } else {
                sorted.sort((a, b) -> Integer.compare(b.part.speed, a.part.speed));
            }

            System.out.print("  Var order: ");
            for (PartVar pv : sorted) System.out.print(pv.part.code + "(" + pv.part.speed + ") ");
            System.out.println();

            IntVar[] orderedVars = sorted.stream().map(pv -> (IntVar) pv.isSelected).toArray(IntVar[]::new);
            model.addDecisionStrategy(orderedVars,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.VariableSelectionStrategy.CHOOSE_FIRST,
                    DecisionStrategyProto.DomainReductionStrategy.SELECT_MAX_VALUE);
        }

        // Solve with FIXED_SEARCH to honor decision strategy
        CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
        solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(true);
        solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);
        if (st != null) {
            solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(SatParameters.SearchBranching.FIXED_SEARCH);
        }

        SolutionCallback cb = new SolutionCallback(partVars);
        CpSolverStatus status = solver.solve(model, cb);

        System.out.println("  Status: " + status);
        for (String s : cb.getSolutions()) {
            System.out.println("  " + s);
        }

        // Verify: first solution should match expectation
        if (st != null && !cb.getSolutions().isEmpty()) {
            String first = cb.getSolutions().get(0);
            if (st == StrategyType.ASCENDING) {
                boolean ok = first.contains("disk1"); // 5400 = slowest/cheapest
                System.out.println("  " + (ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL") + ": expected disk1(speed=5400) first");
            } else {
                boolean ok = first.contains("disk3"); // 9000 = fastest
                System.out.println("  " + (ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL") + ": expected disk3(speed=9000) first");
            }
        }
    }
}

```

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:46:46Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The test works perfectly. The results clearly demonstrate:

**No strategy (baseline)**: Solutions are in solver-default order — disk2(7200) first, then disk3(9000), then disk1(5400).

**ASCENDING speed**: 
- Var order: disk1(5400) → disk2(7200) → disk3(9000)
- All disk1 solutions (qty 1-10) first, then all disk2 solutions, then all disk3 solutions
- First solution: disk1(speed=5400) ✓

**DESCENDING speed**:
- Var order: disk3(9000) → disk2(7200) → disk1(5400)  
- All disk3 solutions (qty 1-10) first, then all disk2 solutions, then all disk1 solutions
- First solution: disk3(speed=9000) ✓

The decision strategy completely controls the solution enumeration order! With `isSelected` BoolVars sorted by attribute and `FIXED_SEARCH`:
- ASC vars + MAX_VALUE → lowest attribute examined first (all its qty variations)
- DESC vars + MAX_VALUE → highest attribute examined first

This is exactly what we need for the production feature. The test file is clean and clearly demonstrates the concept using the project's Part/PartVar model classes.
</details></think>

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-28 23:46:53Z)**_

All three tests pass, clearly demonstrating the strategy:

| Test | First disk found | Speed |
|---|---|---|
| No strategy | disk2 | 7200 (arbitrary) |
| **ASC speed** | disk1 | **5400** (slowest first) |
| **DESC speed** | disk3 | **9000** (fastest first) |

The test at [DecisionStrategyVerifyTest.java](src/test/java/com/jmix/temp3/DecisionStrategyVerifyTest.java) uses `Part` (with `.speed` attribute) and `PartVar` (with `.isSelected` BoolVar) — the same pattern as the production `PartVar` class. Key points:

1. Sort `PartVar.isSelected` BoolVars by `part.speed` (ascending or descending)
2. Pass the sorted array to `model.addDecisionStrategy()` with `CHOOSE_FIRST` + `SELECT_MAX_VALUE`
3. **Must** set `solver.getParameters().setSearchBranching(FIXED_SEARCH)` — without this, CP-SAT ignores the strategy

With ASCENDING, disk1 (speed=5400) is enumerated exhaustively first (all qty values 1-10), then disk2, then disk3. With DESCENDING, the order is reversed.

The user is pointing out two issues:

1. The `assertSoluContain` assertions in SearchStrategyTest are not precise enough - they should check for specific solution content like `"cpu2(Q:1,H:0,S:1)"` rather than just `"cpu2"`.

2. The decision strategy isn't actually working in the production code because `FIXED_SEARCH` is not being set on the solver. The temp3 test proved that `FIXED_SEARCH` is required.

I need to:
1. Fix the production code to set `FIXED_SEARCH` on the solver when decision strategies are present
2. Fix the SearchStrategyTest assertions to be more precise

In `ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl.runInferParas()`:
```java
CpSolver solver = new CpSolver();
solver.getParameters().setEnumerateAllSolutions(req.isEnumerateAllSolution());
solver.getParameters().setNumSearchWorkers(1);

##原生需求
动机    嗯，对部件分类，当前的处理方式是先先根据嗯过滤条件进行分类的过滤，然后再进一步的根据。呃过过滤的部件，构建这个原有的约束表达式。这地方存在一个问题，如果第1步没有部件产生，那么第2步，呃在构建约束表达式的时候，如果这个部件正好和其他的部件分类有关系，那样导致整体没有解。而且没有在最后的结果反馈里面，没有给用户提示说没有找到什么什么什么解，嗯是什么原因导致的？这个处理不合理。我们需要出方案去优化这个这个点。

详细场景呢就是如果这个部件分类，根据输入的过滤条件找找不到，这最后返回结果里面就提示。说根据这个输入x找不到满足条件的部件。但同时其他的能满足要求的还是要继续输出，比方说根据一个输入条件，我有两个部件分类cpu和driverva那个根据driverva的输入条件是没有满足要求的。这个东西，那么在我们的部件分类那个instance上面来输出这个error信息。那么对于其他的比方cpu能满足要求的，那还继续按原有的能输出。嗯。

This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_selection>

动机    嗯，对部件分类，当前的处理方式是先先根据嗯过滤条件进行分类的过滤，然后再进一步的根据。呃过过滤的部件，构建这个原有的约束表达式。这地方存在一个问题，如果第1步没有部件产生，那么第2步，呃在构建约束表达式的时候，如果这个部件正好和其他的部件分类有关系，那样导致整体没有解。而且没有在最后的结果反馈里面，没有给用户提示说没有找到什么什么什么解，嗯是什么原因导致的？这个处理不合理。我们需要出方案去优化这个这个点。

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-29 22:36:44Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants me to write an RFC document in the `doc/` directory about handling the case when a PartCategory filter finds no matching parts. Let me understand the requirement:

1. When a PartConstraintReq's filter condition finds no matching parts in a PartCategory, the current behavior is that the constraint expression is built anyway, which can cause the entire solution to fail.
2. The desired behavior is: when a filter finds no parts for a specific PartCategory, the system should:
   - Report an error/message on that PartCategory's instance saying "no parts found matching condition X"
   - Continue processing other PartCategories that DO have matching parts
   - Still return solutions for the categories that work

Let me first explore the current code to understand the filtering flow, then write the RFC.

Let me look at how `filterClone` and `querySubAtomicParts` work in the current code, and how errors are currently handled.
</details></think>

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-29 22:36:45Z)**_

Let me first explore the current filtering flow to understand the problem.

_**Agent (deepseek-v4-pro 2026-04-29 22:40:12Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="Agent"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Agent**</summary>
Explore filterClone flow

```text
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full PartCategory filtering flow analysis.

---

## PartCategory Filtering Flow in JMix Config Engine

### 1. Two Entry Points That Call `filterClone()`

**Entry A: `ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl.filterClone()`** (when called from `processProduct()`)

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl.java`
- **Lines 172-241**

Inside the loop (line 196-198), when the filter produces **no matching parts**, the code throws an `AlgExecutorException`:

```java
if (filterPartCategory.getAllAtomicPartShortString().isEmpty()) {
    throw new AlgExecutorException(msg);
}
```

The message is: `"Priority-filtered aparts: {} by {}"` -- where `{}` is an empty short string. The exception propagates up to `processProduct()`'s `catch (Exception e)` block (line 166-169) and returns `Result.failed(...)`.

**Entry B: `PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl.doProcess()`** (single-category path)

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl.java`
- **Lines 39-91**

At line 61-63, when the filter produces no matching parts, it returns a **`Result.noSolution(msg)`** instead of throwing:

```java
if (filteredCategory.getAllAtomicPartShortString().isEmpty()) {
    return Result.noSolution(msg);
}
```

The key difference: `ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl` throws an exception (wrapping to `Result.failed`), while `PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl` returns `Result.noSolution()` (code=2).

---

### 2. `PartCategory.filterClone()` --> `querySubAtomicParts()` --> `FilterExpressionExecutor.doSelect()`

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/bmodel/PartCategory.java`

**`filterClone()` (lines 109-131):**

```java
public PartCategory filterClone(PartConstraintReq constraintReq) {
    return filterClone(this, constraintReq, false);
}
```

The private recursive method (lines 113-131) clones the PartCategory, then calls `querySubAtomicParts()` to filter leaf (atomic) parts, then recurses into sub-categories.

**`querySubAtomicParts()` (lines 133-185):**

Two code paths:
- **Non-instance attributes** (line 142-145): calls `FilterExpressionExecutor.doSelect(atomicParts, attrWhereCondition)`.
- **Instance attributes** (line 147-182): iterates each part's `InstanceDynAttrValueItem` list, filters by `doSelect(instValues, attrWhereCondition)`. If the filtered list is empty, skips that part. Otherwise clones the part with aggregated instance values.

**`FilterExpressionExecutor.doSelect()`:**

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/util/FilterExpressionExecutor.java`
- **Lines 111-121 (public entry), 131-152 (core logic)**

```java
public static <T extends IExtensible> List<T> doSelect(List<T> objects, String filterExpr) {
    if (objects == null || objects.isEmpty() || filterExpr == null || filterExpr.trim().isEmpty()) {
        return objects;
    }
    Optional<FilterCondition> filterCondition = parseFilterExpression(filterExpr);
    return doSelect(objects, filterCondition.get());
}
```

When **no parts match**, the core loop (lines 142-152) builds `filterObjects` which ends up as an **empty `ArrayList`**. This empty list is returned to `querySubAtomicParts()`, which adds it to `resultPartCategory` via `addAtomicPartsWithoutStructure()` (an effectively no-op for an empty list).

Then `getAllAtomicPartShortString()` on the filtered PartCategory returns an empty representation, triggering the error guards described in section 1.

---

### 3. `ModuleAlgImpl.initData()` When PartCategory Has Empty Atomic Parts

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleAlgImpl.java`
- **Lines 205-243**

When `initData()` runs (line 237 for `SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl`), it calls `super.initData()` on the base class.

**`ModuleBaseAlgImpl.initData()`:**

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleBaseAlgImpl.java`
- **Lines 657-669**

```java
protected void initData(AlgCPModel model, IModule module, IModuleInput moduleInput, IModuleAlg moduleAlgFile) {
    this.model = model;
    this.module = module;
    this.moduleInput = moduleInput;
    this.compatibleConstraintAlg = new CompatibleConstraintAlg(model);
    initModelAfter(model);
    initAll(moduleAlgFile);  // <-- this is where parts/paras become variables
}
```

**`initAll()` (lines 328-347):**

```java
for (Part part : module.getAtomicParts()) {
    PartVar partVar = initPartVar(part);
    partMap.put(part.getCode(), partVar);
}
```

When the `filteredCategory` has **no atomic parts**, `getAtomicParts()` returns an empty list, so the loop body never executes. `partMap` remains empty. The algorithm proceeds with zero part variables. Later, during `initInput()` (line 159-184 of ModuleAlgImpl), when it tries to add `partEquality` or sum constraints, operations on an empty `partMap` will either be silent no-ops or may produce warnings (e.g., `sumFunConstraint` and `buildSumExpr` may log warnings or produce empty expressions). The CP-SAT solver would then see an under-constrained model which would either be trivially satisfiable or produce an `INFEASIBLE` result depending on other constraints.

---

### 4. `toPartCategoryInput()` and `SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl.initData()` -- Error With Empty FilteredCategory

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/ModuleBaseConstraintExecutorImpl.java`
- **Lines 259-266**

```java
public static PartCategoryInput toPartCategoryInput(PartCategory filteredCategory,
        PartConstraintReq partConstraintReq) {
    PartCategoryInput fromReq = new PartCategoryInput();
    setPartCategoryInputBase(fromReq, partConstraintReq);
    fromReq.setFilteredCategory(filteredCategory);  // stores the empty category directly
    return fromReq;
}
```

`toPartCategoryInput()` itself does NOT validate if `filteredCategory` is empty. It simply wraps it. The problem manifests downstream:

**Flow into `SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl.initData()`:**

1. `PartCategoryInput` (with empty `filteredCategory`) is passed into `ModuleAlgImpl.initData()` (line 237).
2. `SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl.initData()` (line 43-48) calls `super.initData(model, module, moduleInput, moduleAlgFile)` where `module` is the `PartCategory` (the empty filtered one).
3. `ModuleBaseAlgImpl.initData()` -> `initAll()` iterates `module.getAtomicParts()` which is empty -- so zero `PartVar` objects are created.
4. Later, `SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl.initInput()` (line 51-56) calls `setPartCategoryInput(input)`.
5. `setPartCategoryInput()` (ModuleBaseAlgImpl, lines 143-152) calls `setPartCategoryInputVariales()` which looks for constraint parameters. If the `PartConstraintReq` has `attrCode`, `comparator`, `leftValue` set, it will call `sumFunConstraint()`.
6. `sumFunConstraint()` (SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl, lines 124-138) calls `buildSumExpr()` with the part category's empty part list, producing an expression with zero terms. The `ComparisonOperator.applyConstraint` then creates a solver constraint like `0 == leftValue` or `0 >= leftValue`, which may be unsatisfiable.

If there is no `comparator` set, `setPartCategoryInput()` returns early and no error occurs -- the solver will simply produce trivially satisfiable results (any matching solution) since there are no parts to constrain.

---

### 5. `ModuleInst` Error Fields and `SolutionUtils.printSolutions()`

**`ModuleInst`:**

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/cmodel/ModuleInst.java`
- This class has **no error-specific fields**. It extends `ModuleBaseInst` and contains:
  - `id` (Long), `instanceConfigId`, `quantity`, `partCategorys` (List), `priorityAttrValues`, `prioritySortNo`, `priorityOverallValue`
  - No `error`, `errorMessage`, or `status` fields.

**`ModuleBaseInst`:**

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/cmodel/ModuleBaseInst.java`
- Also has **no error fields**. Contains: `instanceId`, `code`, `paras`, `parts`, `extAttrs`, `shortCode`.

**How `SolutionUtils.printSolutions()` reports errors:**

- **File:** `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/impl/SolutionUtils.java`
- **Lines 38-91**

The method receives a `Result<List<ModuleInst>> result` parameter. It prints a "3.Messages:" section (line 86-89) that simply prints `result.getMessage()`, which contains whatever message string was set on the Result:

```java
sb.append("3.Messages:").append(System.lineSeparator());
if (result != null) {
    sb.append(result.getMessage()).append(System.lineSeparator());
}
```

The `Result` class (at `d:/03.code4ccds/jmixconfigengine/src/main/java/com/jmix/executor/model/Result.java`) has three status codes:
- `SUCCESS = 0`
- `FAILED = 1`
- `NO_SOLUTION = 2`

The `message` field on `Result` carries the error text (e.g., "Priority-filtered aparts: {} by {condition}", "no solution", "Failed to process Module constraint: ...", etc.). `SolutionUtils.printSolutions()` does NOT distinguish between result codes -- it always prints the message if it is non-null. The caller must check `result.getCode()` separately.

Error information flows through `Result.code` and `Result.message`, NOT through `ModuleInst` fields. `ModuleInst` has no error-bearing fields at all.

---

### Summary of Key Files and Lines

| File | Key Lines | Role |
|------|-----------|------|
| `.../executor/impl/ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl.java` | 172-241 (`filterClone`), 194-198 (throw on empty), 166-169 (catch) | Entry A: throws `AlgExecutorException` on empty filter |
| `.../executor/impl/PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl.java` | 39-91 (`doProcess`), 61-63 (noSolution on empty) | Entry B: returns `Result.noSolution()` on empty filter |
| `.../executor/bmodel/PartCategory.java` | 109-131 (`filterClone`), 133-185 (`querySubAtomicParts`) | Core filtering logic |
| `.../executor/impl/util/FilterExpressionExecutor.java` | 111-121, 131-152 (`doSelect`) | Filter expression evaluation; returns empty `ArrayList` when no match |
| `.../executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleAlgImpl.java` | 205-243 (`initData`) | Orchestrates PartCategoryAlgImpl creation |
| `.../executor/impl/algmodel/ModuleBaseAlgImpl.java` | 328-347 (`initAll`), 657-669 (`initData`) | Creates PartVar/ParaVar; silently handles empty atomic parts |
| `.../executor/impl/algmodel/SingleInstPartCategoryAlgImpl.java` | 43-48 (`initData`), 51-56 (`initInput`), 124-138 (`sumFunConstraint`) | Single-instance algorithm; builds sum expression from (possibly empty) parts |
| `.../executor/impl/ModuleBaseConstraintExecutorImpl.java` | 259-266 (`toPartCategoryInput`) | Wraps filteredCategory into PartCategoryInput without validating emptiness |
| `.../executor/cmodel/ModuleInst.java` | 1-235 | No error fields |
| `.../executor/model/Result.java` | 1-105 | `code` (0/1/2), `message` -- the only error channel |
| `.../executor/impl/SolutionUtils.java` | 38-91 (`printSolutions`) | Prints `result.getMessage()` in "3.Messages:" section |
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-04-29 22:36:52Z)**_

I need to understand the PartCategory filtering flow in the JMix Config Engine. Specifically:

1. In `ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl.filterClone()`, when `partCategory.filterClone(req)` is called - what happens when the filter condition finds no matching parts?
